digest.component_or_area: authorship attest layer + reducer keyring fold
propositions: Key validity must be anchored to git-commit-ancestry over tickets-branch commit SHAs instead of the HLC-epoch window delivered by e165.
HLC-epoch ordering is author-assignable, letting a malicious clone backdate a revoked-key event to sort before the revocation and verify as valid; commit-ancestry closes this hole.

A new git-aware resolver in authorship.py maps event position prefixes to introducing commit SHAs and derives per-key validity via merge-base --is-ancestor, failing closed (non-verified Verdict, never raises) on any git failure.

The reducer stays pure-filesystem: it folds position-based key records {public_key, added_at, revoked_at} with no git/subprocess and no epoch fields, while the git-aware resolver lives in the attest layer.
Intra-commit refinement uses the {HLC-ts}-{uuid} position total order within a single batched commit to order key add/revoke against the event when they share a commit.

The HLC-epoch API (verify_authorship_at_epoch, epoch_for_position, keys_valid_at_epoch, keyring_epoch/added_epoch/revoked_epoch) is removed and e165's epoch tests are replaced with commit-ancestry tests; schema and reducer cache versions are bumped with a defensive v4 snapshot migration.
